articles.title: TESTING
articles.summary: If slow QA processes bottleneck you or your software engineering team and you're releasing slower because of it — you need to check out QA Wolf.

articles.title: Schedule a demo to learn more
PREDICTIVE CI: WHY SMART TEAMS ARE ALREADY MAKING THE SWITCH
articles.summary: AI coding agents are rapidly increasing code generation, shifting engineers' focus to reviewing AI-written code, which is more challenging than writing it. Current testing methods struggle to address the nuances and potential issues in AI-generated code. Predictive CI uses AI to proactively generate tests, identify regressions, and suggest fixes, which leads to better code reliability and reduces engineer workload.

articles.title: BOOKMARKABLE BY DESIGN: URL-DRIVEN STATE IN HTMX
ENOUGH AI COPILOTS! WE NEED AI HUDS
articles.summary: The prevalent "AI copilot" model is old. Instead, design "AI HUDs" like the Head-Up Display in airplanes or spellcheck. These focus on human awareness and understanding through subtle, integrated information, offering new senses and insights.

articles.title: ON THE EDGE OF COMPETENCE
articles.summary: The importance of understanding and expanding one's "circle of competence" is necessary for becoming a better engineer. The circle itself represents fluency in a particular domain, and maintaining a sharp circle happens through continuous learning, revisiting fundamentals, tracking decisions, and seeking feedback.

articles.title: INTRODUCING STUDY MODE
articles.summary: OpenAI introduced "study mode" in ChatGPT, a new learning experience to guide users through problems step-by-step rather than providing immediate answers. This feature uses custom system instructions to encourage active participation, manage cognitive load, and promote self-reflection.

articles.title: GLASS3D GENERATOR
articles.summary: A simple code generator for creating 3D glass effects using CSS.

articles.title: VISUAL CSS
articles.summary: Visual CSS is an AI-powered styling tool that allows for real-time visual editing of any website's CSS.

articles.title: A FULL CODE AGENT IN 200 LINES—HERE'S HOW
articles.summary: A minimal code agent, dubbed "nano-claude-code" in this article, can be built in just 200 lines of code using JavaScript and Bun. The agent uses tools like reading and writing files and executing bash commands to interact with the local filesystem. The core of the agent is a while loop that interacts with a large language model to plan and execute coding tasks based on user prompts and tool availability.

articles.title: THE MAKING OF DARIO AMODEI
articles.summary: Dario Amodei is the CEO of Anthropic. His commitment stems from the loss of his father, who died from an illness that became curable shortly after his death, which fuels his desire to accelerate technological progress. He founded Anthropic after leaving OpenAI with the goal of developing leading large language models with a strong emphasis on safety and ethical practices.

articles.title: A PERIODIC TABLE OF SYSTEM DESIGN PRINCIPLES
articles.summary: A "periodic table" of recurring system design principles from various domains in computer systems, with the aim of creating a shared vocabulary for reasoning about structure, trade-offs, and design choices.

articles.title: WHEN JAVASCRIPT DECIDED MY DAY STARTS AT 9AM
articles.summary: A bug caused by JavaScript's `new Date('YYYY-MM-DD')` constructor interprets the date as midnight UTC, leading to unexpected filtering behavior in a Japanese admin dashboard where data before 9 AM was excluded due to the UTC+9 timezone.

articles.title: SIMPLE LIVE RELOAD FOR DEVELOPING STATIC SITES
articles.summary: A 39-line client-side JavaScript snippet can automatically reload a static website in the browser whenever changes are detected in the source files, using HTTP HEAD requests to monitor Last-Modified and ETag headers.

articles.title: MEASURING ENGINEERING
articles.summary: Instead of focusing on individual developer metrics like cycle time, improving software delivery velocity requires a systems-level approach that considers team trends, system improvements, and the inherent variability of software development.
